description: Hand-labelled semantic-retrieval set (the eval harness, crates/b2-embed/evals/). Each query avoids the target note's keywords (synonyms/paraphrase) so it measures semantic lift, not lexical overlap. `relevant` lists the vault-relative path(s) that should rank first. The corpus clusters notes into confusable topic groups (coffee, sleep, plants, security, geology, cycling), so ranking WITHIN a cluster is tested, not just across unrelated topics. An optional `passage` is a short verbatim phrase from the target passage: those queries are additionally scored at CHUNK level (does the ranked chunk's text contain the phrase?), which is what makes chunking changes visible to the eval (index-engine.md, GH #44). Keep passages verbatim, short, and unique within the corpus.

queries.query: how do leaves turn light into food
pedalling a bike up a steep hill
i can't fall asleep at night
a mountain that erupts and spews magma
keeping messages secret from eavesdroppers
a strong little italian coffee shot
scrambling data with a secret key
trouble sleeping because of caffeine
steeping coarse grounds then pushing the screen down
turning green beans brown in a heated rotating drum
a consistent bedtime routine in a dark cool room
why sleeping minds play vivid stories
my potted fern's leaves are going brown indoors
the underground parts that anchor a plant and drink for it
is a long random phrase safer than a short complex one
an urgent email pretending to be my bank
why the ground shakes when a fault lets go
slow rivers of ice grinding out valleys
squeaky chain and sloppy gear changes
how warm should the crock stay for sour cabbage
why the flour and water culture needs regular feeding
protecting sore rubbed heels on a long trail walk
how heavy is a minimalist pack before food and water
setting aside savings for a surprise car repair
a retirement account whose withdrawals are never taxed
